Operation 'listFeeds' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. The current description mentions pagination support but doesn't explain the pagination parameters or what the response contains. Added clarity about pagination parameters and response structure to help AI agents understand how to use pagination and what data will be returned.

data.current_description: Retrieve all RSS feeds associated with the authenticated account, with pagination support.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve all RSS feeds associated with the authenticated account. Supports pagination through limit and offset parameters. Returns a paginated list of Feed objects containing feed metadata, source URLs, and configuration details.
data.reasoning: The current description mentions pagination support but doesn't explain the pagination parameters or what the response contains. Added clarity about pagination parameters and response structure to help AI agents understand how to use pagination and what data will be returned.

message: Operation 'createFeed' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is generic. Description lacks clarity about input requirements (CreateFeedRequest), the idempotent behavior (returns existing feed), and what the response contains. Enhanced summary to be more specific about the action, and expanded description to clarify inputs, the deduplication behavior, and response structure for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Generate a new RSS feed from a given URL. Supports HTML pages, Facebook, YouTube, and other sources. If a feed with the same configuration already exists, the existing feed data is returned.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate RSS feed from URL source
data.description_suggestion: Generate a new RSS feed from a specified URL source. Supports multiple source types including HTML pages, Facebook, YouTube, and other platforms. Accepts configuration parameters via CreateFeedRequest body. If a feed with identical configuration already exists, returns the existing feed instead of creating a duplicate. Returns the created or existing Feed object with assigned feed ID and metadata.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description lacks clarity about input requirements (CreateFeedRequest), the idempotent behavior (returns existing feed), and what the response contains. Enhanced summary to be more specific about the action, and expanded description to clarify inputs, the deduplication behavior, and response structure for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'deleteFeed' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description lacks input parameter details and response information. The warning about API key blocking is important but unclear. Enhanced description to specify that the feed ID is required as a path parameter, clarify that this is a permanent deletion, explain the response, and reframe the abuse warning more clearly as a rate-limiting/blocking consequence to help AI agents understand the operational constraints.

data.current_description: Remove a specified RSS feed. Repeated creation and deletion of identical feeds may result in API key blocking.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently remove a specified RSS feed by its ID. This operation deletes the feed and all associated data from the account. Requires the feed ID as a path parameter. Returns a success confirmation. Note: Repeated rapid creation and deletion of identical feeds may result in API key rate limiting or blocking as a abuse prevention measure.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks input parameter details and response information. The warning about API key blocking is important but unclear. Enhanced description to specify that the feed ID is required as a path parameter, clarify that this is a permanent deletion, explain the response, and reframe the abuse warning more clearly as a rate-limiting/blocking consequence to help AI agents understand the operational constraints.
